metrics.narrative: No dollar-quantified ROI or savings case has ever been produced. On the 19 Aug 2025 demo Amresh Munshi described qualitative pain (tail-spend under $25k-$50k managed entirely by email with no audit trail, a recent security breach where a ~$10,000 tool was purchased below the review threshold without a required TSR check) and named negotiation guardrails (a Net 45-60 payment-term target, a 3% annual cost-increase ceiling with 3-5% maximum negotiation range) but never a top-line savings target or business case tied to the $185,000 investment. Quantifying this into a written ROI model is the open move.

champion.narrative: Amresh Munshi is the champion: he drove the 19 Aug 2025 session, called the demo "really exciting," said he was "impressed" with the materials, and committed to bringing in his procurement ops team, power users, and Chris Soriano/Alycia Stafford for a September 2025 follow-up. However, that follow-up never materialized, and Amresh has not replied to any of five outreach attempts since 26 Feb 2026 (131 days). What developed: early, genuine enthusiasm and process ownership. What is missing: any recent reconfirmation, and any secondary relationship with Purav Desai, Chris Soriano, Alycia Stafford, or Justin Robinson beyond cc-lines, leaving the deal single-threaded and the champion now at risk.

competition.narrative: Salesforce lists an active multi-vendor bake-off against GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba, consistent with Amresh's 19 Aug 2025 statement that Cornerstone would down-select to "one or two" finalists for a POC by Dec 2025/Jan 2026. That checkpoint has passed with zero visibility into where Zycus stands. Oracle iProcurement remains the incumbent ERP-integrated procure-to-pay tool and will not be replaced (Zycus is positioned as an overlay, not a rip-and-replace). Coupa was removed from Cornerstone's stack in a 2021 leadership decision unrelated to this evaluation and is not a current rival. The wedge, per Amresh, is a complete source-to-pay-plus-agentic-AI package versus point solutions: "intake is a word... abused out there... you have lot of intake providers, but they will also give you small solutions."

identify_pain.narrative: Amresh Munshi articulated specific, credible pain on the 19 Aug 2025 call: tail-spend purchases are handled entirely outside any system, via email, with "no audit trail"; Oracle iProcurement cannot be extended for autonomous sourcing without "very significantly high cost"; and a recent example where a ~$10,000 add-on tool was purchased below the review threshold without the required TSR check, which he called "a security breach." He owns this pain directly as the project lead driving the evaluation.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or legal engagement has started. Cornerstone's own contract/legal review, InfoSec, and IT workflows run through ServiceNow internally, but no scoping conversation between Zycus and the buyer's legal/InfoSec team has occurred. Visibility would come from re-engaging Purav Desai or Chris Soriano to ask what internal procurement/legal process Zycus would need to enter once a vendor is selected.

economic_buyer.narrative: Amresh Munshi named the approver explicitly on the 19 Aug 2025 call: "finally, my head of global procurement to be part of it to give his blessing and take it forward." That person has never been named, never appeared on a call, and no budget or pricing conversation has started. Amresh positioned himself as project lead controlling access ("I don't want to engage anybody unless I don't see value"), so the economic buyer's path currently runs entirely through a champion who has gone unresponsive for 131+ days.

decision_process.narrative: Amresh Munshi laid out an explicit process on 19 Aug 2025: vendor exploration through Sept 2025, down-select and pricing discussions after Sept 2025, one or two finalists into a POC by Dec 2025/Jan 2026, contracting to follow the POC (with a ~1-month gap to final signature), and implementation targeted for a go-live "max to max" of May 2026, with a more conservative ~1-year (mid-2027) internal target. That process has since broken: the deal was demoted from Shortlisted back to the relevant stakeholder on 14 Apr 2026, indicating the down-select did not conclude as planned, and there has been no update on where Cornerstone landed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria surfaced on the 19 Aug 2025 call: an overlay solution (not a replacement) for Oracle iProcurement/Fusion ERP, SSO via Okta, configurable autonomous-sourcing dollar thresholds (currently $50,000, target back to $25,000), a mandatory TSR/security review gate for new technology purchases below threshold, and strategic-sourcing analytics with pre-negotiation insights and historical benchmarking (Amresh's own example: renegotiating Okta). None of these have been re-confirmed or formally documented in an RFP/BRD since.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Where Zycus demoed, fit was strong; where scoping needs to be formalized (thresholds, security workflow, deep analytics), fit remains conceptually validated but unconfirmed for over 10 months.

hard.ais_why:
• Champion Amresh Munshi engaged the August 19 2025 demo squarely on autonomous AI. The call confirmed strong interest in autonomous sourcing and AI-enabled negotiation, with explicit configurable thresholds for purchases below $25,000. He set savings expectations of 4 to 9 percent from autonomous negotiation, framing AI as the value driver rather than a feature on the wishlist.
• Amresh and ops lead Purav Desai treated AI as the core architecture. The integration requirement was that Zycus deliver an overlay solution on Oracle iProcurement, Oracle Fusion and ServiceNow without ripping iProcurement, with mandatory TSR review automation for sub-$25K critical software. That is buyer-led specification of where AI must plug in.
• Project sequencing reinforces the Hungry call: exploration through September, POC in December or January, go-live targeted May 2026 within roughly a year. Amresh is convening additional stakeholders including Chris and Alicia and power users. Two recorded sessions, one completed and one cancelled, plus action items committing both sides to AI-anchored next steps.
